title: Grade 7 General Science — Units 3-7
description: 50 challenging questions covering elements/compounds, cells, living things diversity, Earth in space, and motion/force/energy.

questions.question: In the expression 4NH₃, what does the coefficient 4 indicate according to the textbook?
The chemical symbol for silver is Ag. From which Latin name is this symbol derived?
In the LCM method of balancing 4Al + 3O₂ → 2Al₂O₃, the total valence numbers are 3, 4, and 6. What is their LCM used to find the coefficients?
How many oxygen atoms are present in one formula unit of Ca(NO₃)₂?
When balancing the equation N₂ + H₂ → NH₃ by the inspection method, which coefficient is placed before H₂?
Mixing silver nitrate and sodium chloride solutions produces a white precipitate. What is this precipitate?
Elements such as hydrogen, nitrogen, and oxygen exist as diatomic molecules. Which of the following exists as a monoatomic gas?
Which three elements combine to form magnesium carbonate?
What is the correct chemical formula for Iron (III) chloride?
In naming simple binary compounds, the ending of the non-metal name is replaced with which suffix?
In the notation 2Fe, what is the number 2 called?
In the synthesis of sulfuric acid, sulfur is oxidized to sulfur dioxide and then to sulfur trioxide. Which gas is bubbled through water to produce the acid?
A compound microscope has an eyepiece labeled 10X and a high-power objective labeled 40X. What is the total magnification?
Robert Hooke observed cork cells in 1665. What did Anton van Leeuwenhoek observe in 1674 that Hooke had not?
According to the textbook, what is the maximum ATP yield from one glucose molecule during aerobic cellular respiration?
Both lactic acid fermentation in animal cells and ethanol fermentation in yeast produce how many ATP per glucose molecule?
The inner mitochondrial membrane has folds called cristae. What is the function of these folds?
Which organelle is responsible for the sorting, tagging, packaging, and distribution of lipids and proteins?
The human egg cell measures about 0.12 mm in diameter, while Mycoplasma gallicepticum measures about 0.0001 mm. Approximately how many Mycoplasma cells could fit across the diameter of one egg cell?
When focusing a specimen on a compound microscope, with which objective lens should you always start?
In animal cells, which organelle is described as the cell's 'garbage disposal' because it contains digestive enzymes?
The five-kingdom classification system was developed by which scientist and in which year?
A horse and a donkey can produce a mule, but the mule is infertile. According to the textbook, what is the reason?
options: It belongs to a different genus
It is a product of organisms of different species
questions.correctAnswer: It is a product of organisms of different species

questions.question: According to the textbook, how many pairs of antennae do insects have?
If two organisms belong to the same order, which higher-level group must they also share?
What is the key difference between gymnosperms and angiosperms regarding their seeds?
options: Angiosperm seeds are not enclosed in fruit
Gymnosperm seeds are not enclosed in fruit
questions.correctAnswer: Gymnosperm seeds are not enclosed in fruit

questions.question: Which kingdom consists of prokaryotic unicellular organisms that lack membrane-bound organelles and reproduce by binary fission?
Bryophytes (mosses and liverworts) lack vascular tissue. What is the main consequence of this?
options: They cannot transport water and nutrients efficiently
questions.correctAnswer: They cannot transport water and nutrients efficiently

questions.question: Cyanobacteria (blue-green algae) hold an important place in Earth's history. What were they the first organisms to produce as a byproduct of photosynthesis?
In the scientific name Panthera leo, what taxonomic level does 'Panthera' represent?
Which class of vertebrates is poikilothermic (cold-blooded), has dry skin with scales, and lays eggs with a rubbery waterproof shell on land?
Animals store carbon as glycogen and fat. Which other kingdom also stores carbon as glycogen?
The fungus Penicillium was used to produce which important medical substance?
Earth's equatorial circumference is 40,075 km and its polar circumference is 40,008 km. What is the difference between these two measurements?
An observer at sea notices a ship appears to 'sink' as it sails over the horizon. What does this observation best demonstrate?
options: The Earth has a curved surface
questions.correctAnswer: The Earth has a curved surface

questions.question: What is the precise time Earth takes to complete one full rotation on its axis?
In the nitrogen cycle, which step is NOT completed by bacteria?
Which layer of the atmosphere contains about 75% of all the air and almost all of the water vapor?
What causes the temperature to increase with height in the stratosphere?
options: Absorption of ultraviolet radiation by ozone
questions.correctAnswer: Absorption of ultraviolet radiation by ozone

questions.question: The lithosphere includes the crust and which other part of Earth?
Around 500 B.C., Pythagoras proposed a spherical Earth. What was the basis for his proposal according to the textbook?
options: Aesthetic grounds rather than physical evidence
questions.correctAnswer: Aesthetic grounds rather than physical evidence

questions.question: The stratosphere extends to about 50 km above Earth. What is the approximate elevation of the stratopause (the upper boundary of the stratosphere)?
A ball thrown vertically upward slows down, stops, and falls back to Earth. Which fundamental force causes this behavior?
Earth's revolution around the Sun follows a path. According to the textbook's classification, this motion is an example of which type?
According to the textbook, a battery is a transducer that converts chemical energy into what form?
What happens to the spring in a spring balance when a larger force is applied to the hook?
According to the textbook, which type of energy source has a higher upfront cost but lower carbon emissions and carbon footprint?
What is the scientific instrument called that is used to measure force?
Which of the following is listed in the textbook as a cause of resource depletion?
The textbook defines a transducer as a device used for what purpose?
options: To transform energy from one form to another
To measure the size of a force
questions.correctAnswer: To transform energy from one form to another
